stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains, including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of 'llama' and 'facebook' tags indicates relevance to major LLM developments from Meta.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies, including transformers and safetensors.
The focus is on text-generation capabilities within the context of the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic has released Claude Fable 5, its newest and most powerful widely available AI model.
The model demonstrates exceptional performance across software engineering, knowledge work, and vision tasks.
Anthropic claims Fable 5's lead over competitors increases with the complexity and length of tasks.

stories.title: Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Apple is integrating new generative AI photo editing tools into its ecosystem.
The announcement at WWDC 2026 signals a shift in Apple's stance on photo realism.
These new features grant users powerful, effortless image manipulation capabilities.

stories.title: Meta will use your activity on other websites to personalize your feeds
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Meta is expanding its data usage to incorporate activity from external websites and businesses.
The collected off-platform data will be used to personalize user feeds and AI responses.
This builds upon Meta's existing practice of using third-party data for ad targeting.

stories.title: One day after discovery, Meta pulls facial recognition code from its smart glasses
stories.author: Dhruv Mehrotra and Dell Cameron, wired.com
bullet_points: Meta removed facial recognition code from its smart glasses shortly after its discovery.
The company has not provided an official reason for the removal or indicated if the feature will return.
This action highlights ongoing privacy concerns and regulatory scrutiny surrounding facial recognition technology.

stories.title: Apple says its AI is still private, even when it's running on Google's servers
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Apple is implementing AI features that run on Google's cloud infrastructure.
Crucially, Apple maintains that these AI processes remain private, preventing Google from accessing user data.
This highlights a significant industry trend toward privacy-preserving cloud computing for AI.

stories.title: Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP)
stories.author: Philip Black
bullet_points: Foundation models are evolving beyond simple response generation into complex operational roles.
Future AI deployments involve multi-human, multi-agent collaborations across organizational boundaries.
Agents are increasingly responsible for high-stakes tasks, including customer interactions, code generation, and clinical decisions.

stories.title: Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
stories.author: raffael_de
bullet_points: Microsoft's open-source tools were compromised in a security breach.
The attack specifically targeted and resulted in the theft of passwords belonging to AI developers.
This highlights significant security vulnerabilities within the open-source ecosystem used by AI professionals.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on model deployment and performance.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing performance on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating early adoption of this performance enhancement.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Apple wants Europe to blink
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly delaying the rollout of its new AI-powered Siri in Europe.
The delay is linked to regulatory hurdles or requirements from the European Union (EU).
The news highlights ongoing friction between major tech companies and EU digital regulations regarding AI implementation.

stories.title: NASA assigns crew for Artemis III, sets aggressive timeline for flying it
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has assigned the crew for the Artemis III mission.
The mission is set to fly on an aggressive timeline.
Artemis III is highlighted as a major demonstration of technological capability.

stories.title: Drone boat picked up downed US Army helicopter pilots—a first for sea rescues
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The US Navy successfully conducted a sea rescue using a drone boat.
This marks a first-time application of drone technology for rescuing downed US Army helicopter pilots at sea.
The operation took place near the Strait of Hormuz under Task Force 59.

stories.title: High-severity vulnerability in Linux caused by a single faulty character
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A high-severity vulnerability was discovered in the Linux operating system.
The bug is a use-after-free vulnerability, which can be exploited to bypass sandbox security measures.
The vulnerability was traced back to a single faulty character, highlighting the potential impact of subtle coding errors.

stories.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
stories.author: Ruslan Salakhutdinov
bullet_points: Introduces iOSWorld, a novel benchmark designed to test the 'personal intelligence' of phone agents.
The benchmark simulates a persistent user identity across 26 native iOS apps, allowing agents to reason over personal data (transactions, messages, etc.).
Addresses a gap in current mobile agent benchmarks by moving beyond impersonal, isolated instruction sets to personalized context.

stories.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
stories.author: Ruslan Salakhutdinov
bullet_points: Introduces iOSWorld, a novel benchmark designed to test the intelligence of phone agents.
The benchmark emphasizes 'personal intelligence,' requiring agents to reason over a user's persistent identity, history, and preferences across multiple apps.
iOSWorld is built within a native iOS simulator environment featuring 26 interconnected apps with real-world data (transactions, messages).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: Microsoft AI chief walks back comments about AI taking over white-collar work
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft AI chief Mustafa Suleyman retracted previous comments suggesting AI would fully automate white-collar jobs.
Suleyman clarified that AI's role is to assist workers (e.g., lawyers, accountants) in completing specific tasks, not replace them entirely.
The discussion highlights the current debate surrounding AI's impact on professional, knowledge-based employment.

stories.title: First Drive: The 2027 Rivian R2 entirely changes the EV game
stories.author: Michael Teo Van Runkle
bullet_points: Rivian is launching its second electric vehicle, the R2.
The R2 is positioned as a sub-$60,000 model.
The announcement suggests the R2 could significantly impact the EV market.

stories.title: FCC lifts looming deadline for Amazon Leo satellite broadband constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The FCC has granted a waiver concerning Amazon Leo's satellite broadband constellation.
The waiver's justification is framed as serving the public interest by fostering competition.
This development impacts the deployment timeline for a second major satellite broadband provider.

stories.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
stories.author: Xiaojuan Qi
bullet_points: Introduces OmniGameArena, a new, unified benchmark specifically designed for evaluating Vision-Language Model (VLM) agents in interactive game environments.
Addresses limitations in existing benchmarks, such as focusing only on single first-attempt scores and lacking protocols for heterogeneous agent classes.
The benchmark utilizes twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games to provide a more comprehensive and dynamic evaluation framework for VLM agents.

stories.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
stories.author: Irene Solaiman
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of inconsistent and uninterpretable reporting of AI evaluation results across various platforms.
Proposes 'Evaluation Cards' as an interpretive layer to standardize and unify AI evaluation reporting.
Aims to solve gaps where existing methods only cover narrow evaluation lifecycle slices and lack composability.

stories.title: Topological Neural Operators
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: Introduces Topological Neural Operators (TNOs) for operator learning on cell complexes.
Lifts Neural Operators (NOs) from point/edge functions to topological domains.
Uses Discrete Exterior Calculus to model interactions and enable explicit cross-dimensional coupling.

stories.title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality
stories.author: Ladan Tahvildari
bullet_points: Addresses the reliability issues (LLM hallucinations, error propagation) in multi-agent code generation systems.
Introduces Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy (FASE) to quantify uncertainty in code generation.
Aims to improve upon existing methods by reducing the reliance on costly, LLM-driven equivalence checks.

stories.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution
stories.author: Yifan Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in AI safety: current reliance on downstream hard safety filters can mask poor performance of the upstream learned controller.
It proposes a framework to determine 'who earns the safety'—whether the policy itself or its protective layers are responsible for constraint satisfaction.
The research introduces 'Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control' and 'Safety Attribution' to measure safety contributions accurately.

stories.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: The research addresses the difficulty domain scientists face in configuring specialized scientific simulators.
It frames simulator setup as an 'agent-tool interface grounding' problem for coding agents.
The goal is to determine minimal, simulator-specific adaptations needed for general coding agents to interact with complex scientific software.

stories.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
stories.author: Tianyu Pang
bullet_points: The paper addresses challenges in Reinforcement Learning (RL) applied to Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically focusing on post-training optimization.
It critiques mainstream RL methods (like PPO/GRPO) for using ratio-clipping, arguing that the importance ratio is insufficient for capturing distributional shift in large vocabularies.
The research suggests rethinking divergence regularization to improve the stability and effectiveness of LLM RL, referencing recent work like DPPO.

stories.title: Topological Neural Operators
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: Introduces Topological Neural Operators (TNOs) to extend neural operators beyond point/edge data.
TNOs operate on cell complexes, representing data across various dimensional features.
Utilizes Discrete Exterior Calculus to model interactions and enable explicit cross-dimensional coupling (gradient, curl, divergence).

stories.title: Zero Touch Predictive Orchestration: Automating Time-Series Models for the Cloud-Edge Continuum
stories.author: Raymond Knopp
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing volatile Cloud-Edge Continuum (CEC) environments using predictive orchestration.
Proposes a solution to the 'cold start' problem in localized time-series forecasting for new edge nodes.
Focuses on fully automated systems to handle unique hardware and microservice behaviors across distributed infrastructure.

stories.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
stories.author: Martin Schrimpf
bullet_points: Introduces 'Topo-Omni,' a new deep topographic multimodal model for brain analysis.
Addresses limitations of previous models which were unimodal and failed to capture contiguous cortical processing streams.
Aims to map and understand functionally selective brain regions by integrating visual, auditory, and language/cognitive data.

stories.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
stories.author: Arjun Mukherjee
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of data scarcity in Neural Machine Translation (NMT) for low-resource Indigenous languages.
Proposes a data synthesis methodology, using community-sourced dictionaries to create a large synthetic corpus, avoiding harmful web-scraping.
Applies Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) via LoRA adapters on mT5 for efficient model training on Q'eqchi' Mayan.

stories.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of adapting general coding agents to specialized input languages of scientific simulators.
Frames simulator setup as an 'agent-tool interface grounding' problem to minimize domain scientist effort.
Proposes developing 'Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters' to bridge the gap between general AI and specific scientific software.

stories.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
stories.author: Arjun Mukherjee
bullet_points: Addresses the critical challenge of data scarcity in Neural Machine Translation (NMT) for low-resource Indigenous languages like Q'eqchi' Mayan.
Proposes a novel data synthesis methodology to create large training corpora from community-sourced dictionaries, avoiding problematic web-scraping.
Employs Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) using LoRA adapters on mT5 for efficient model adaptation.

stories.title: Multi-Turn Evaluation of Deep Research Agents Under Process-Level Feedback
stories.author: Jeff Z. Pan
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current deep research agent (DRA) benchmarks, which only evaluate single-shot outputs.
It introduces a multi-turn evaluation framework to test how DRAs improve reports based on feedback.
The study compares two feedback mechanisms: self-reflection and targeted process-level guidance on research strategy.

stories.title: The Neutral Mask: How RLHF Provides Shallow Alignment while Leaving Partisan Structure Intact in a Large Language Model
stories.author: Wendy K. Tam
bullet_points: The paper investigates the limitations of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) in achieving deep alignment in LLMs.
It suggests that RLHF may only enforce 'functional compliance' rather than truly embedding desired 'human values'.
The research offers a mechanistic analysis into how RLHF maintains underlying partisan structures despite alignment training.

stories.title: IS-CoT: Breaking the Long-form Generation Collapse via Interleaved Structural Thinking
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the persistent challenge of generating coherent and controllable long-form content with LLMs.
Identifies a 'length collapse' issue where reasoning-enhanced models fail sharply beyond ~2,000 words in open-ended writing.
Proposes 'IS-CoT' (Interleaved Structural Thinking) to overcome the limitations of static hierarchical planning in long-form generation.

stories.title: Learning to Attack and Defend: Adaptive Red Teaming of Language Models via GRPO
stories.author: Mark Russinovich
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for adaptive AI red teaming to counter evolving language model attacks.
It introduces AdvGRPO, a novel co-training framework designed to stabilize GRPO for joint attacker-defender optimization.
The research builds upon existing work using PPO and DPO but solves the instability issues associated with GRPO in this context.

stories.title: Apple decided not to roll out Siri in EU after denied request for exemption
stories.author: flanged
bullet_points: Apple has chosen not to implement Siri in the European Union.
The decision followed a denial of an exemption request by Apple.
This highlights ongoing regulatory hurdles for major tech companies in the EU.

stories.title: Ultrafast machine learning on FPGAs via Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
stories.author: ag2718
bullet_points: The article discusses implementing ultrafast machine learning models on Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs).
The core technology leveraged is the use of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs).
This represents a niche but significant advancement in accelerating ML inference/training via specialized hardware.

stories.title: Build a Realtime Translation App with Gemini Live API, LiveKit, & Google Cloud Run
stories.author: thorwebdev
bullet_points: The article details building a real-time translation application.
It leverages modern technologies including the Gemini Live API, LiveKit, and Google Cloud Run.
The focus is on enabling seamless, real-time cross-language communication.
